scenario: Beacon Logistics runs Camunda 8.8 for a routing platform with 4000 shipments per day. A team lead wants to retire an unused process definition version from Operate and asks the operator how progress of the delete is tracked once the confirmation dialog has been accepted on the Processes page.
question: Where does the Processes-page delete-resources flow tell the operator the progress of the delete operation can be seen?

options.text: In a toast notification at the top of the Operate dashboard that automatically disappears after 30 seconds once the delete operation reaches the cluster broker
In an audit log section on the Console cluster detail page, where every delete operation is recorded by deployment id with a separate tab for the operator role
In a Tasklist row created for the operator after confirmation, where the delete operation is completed by claiming and completing the task within 1 hour
In the Operations panel on the right side of the screen in Operate, where the progress of the delete operation appears as a tracked entry after the confirmation dialog

a.text: Incorrect. The userguide does not rely on a 30 seconds toast notification on the dashboard; the documented progress view is the Operations panel on the right side of the screen in Operate.

b.text: Incorrect. Console is not the documented location for the progress of this Operate delete operation; the flow keeps the operator inside Operate and tracks progress in the Operations panel.

c.text: Incorrect. The delete flow is performed inside Operate and is not routed through Tasklist; the operator watches progress in the Operations panel on the right side of the screen.

d.text: Correct. Step 4 of Delete process definition from Processes page states that the progress of the delete operation can be seen in the Operations panel on the right side of the screen in Operate, after the operator confirms the dialog.

explanation: After confirming the delete on the Processes page, the operator tracks progress in the Operations panel on the right side of the Operate screen. That panel records the delete operation and shows its progression, providing a single place in Operate to observe the lifecycle of the delete request.
